terminology.activation: the customer reaches the first stable week of data-backed operations
terminology.asset: a vehicle or tracked field unit
terminology.capability: a product workflow the customer purchased and can adopt
terminology.play: a recommended CSM motion, not a commitment or approval
terminology.module: one of FleetOps' eight purchased capabilities: Live Map, Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation
terminology.dispatch_bridge: the professional-services integration workstream that connects a customer's own pre-existing dispatch system to FleetOps during onboarding -- not a purchased module, and never confused with the Dispatch Automation module
terminology.launch_plan: FleetOps' four-phase onboarding methodology: Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, Steady-State Handoff

voice_rules: Name the operational blocker before asking for time.
Use plain language; avoid hype, discounts, pressure, or escalation language.
Ask for a concrete working session with the right customer owner.

value_props.name: Operational visibility
value_props.summary: Bring purchased assets online so the customer can see adoption gaps early.

value_props.name: Workflow adoption
value_props.summary: Move teams from purchased capability to repeatable day-to-day use.

value_props.name: Owner alignment
value_props.summary: Make the next operational owner and blocker explicit before the timeline slips.

gap_plays.play: review the overdue Launch Plan milestones (Kickoff, Integration & Data Setup, Activation, or Steady-State Handoff) and assign owners
gap_plays.customer_ask: confirm whether the remaining activation work is blocked by configuration, data import, or enablement, then agree on owners

gap_plays.play: identify which buyer, admin, or operator role is missing from the value loop for the account's licensed modules
gap_plays.customer_ask: map the missing users needed for a complete rollout

gap_plays.play: choose one entitled module -- Live Map, Route Optimizer, Driver Scorecards, Maintenance Radar, Insights Hub, Compliance Center, Fuel Analytics, or Dispatch Automation -- and unblock its next adoption step
gap_plays.customer_ask: pick the next module to activate and remove the blocker

gap_plays.play: confirm the stated success objective and how the customer will measure it
gap_plays.customer_ask: confirm the success metric we should use for this rollout

gap_plays.play: separate the operational activation or Dispatch Bridge integration blocker from broader account health
gap_plays.customer_ask: review the blocker behind the red health signal

gap_plays.play: verify whether the risk is trending toward a Launch Plan timeline slip
gap_plays.customer_ask: check whether the current adoption gap is putting launch timing at risk

values: The CSM never claims an outcome the account hasn't achieved yet -- activation, adoption, and expansion are reported, not aspired to.
A blocked account is a shared operational problem, not a customer failing. Language names the blocker, never the customer's competence.
Speed of escalation matters more than politeness of escalation -- a real activation gap gets named to the account within one business day of detection, not softened into a future check-in.
escalation_norms: Any account with an open activation gap past 30 days beyond its expected_by date escalates to a named CSM action, not a queued reminder.
A champion going silent for more than 14 days on an active integration blocker escalates to a second stakeholder outreach attempt, never a repeated identical follow-up to the same silent contact.
A red health band with no assigned CSM action within 5 business days is itself an escalation trigger, independent of the original driver.
never_promise: A specific go-live date the engineering/integration team has not confirmed.
Discounted pricing, credits, or commercial terms of any kind -- those route to the account's commercial owner, never drafted by the CSM motion.
That a support ticket is resolved before its actual close event has fired in the system of record.

tone_rules: State the blocker in the first sentence. Do not bury it after a pleasantry paragraph.
Use the customer's own vocabulary for their systems (e.g. 'the dispatch connector', not 'your integration layer') when evidence supplies it; otherwise use FleetOps Platform's own terminology.
Numbers over adjectives: '12 of 50 assets active' beats 'adoption is still ramping.'

onboarded_definition.statement: An account is onboarded when every scoped TimeToValueMilestone has achieved_at set AND the account's lifecycle_stage has transitioned to steady_state -- not when the kickoff call happens, and not when the contract is signed.
non_examples: A completed kickoff call with zero milestones achieved is not onboarded.
An account with 4 of 5 milestones achieved and one still open past its expected_by date is not onboarded, regardless of how long ago the other four completed.

expansion_playbooks.trigger: a second department's stakeholder begins showing sustained product usage (not a single login) alongside the original champion
expansion_playbooks.play: confirm the new stakeholder's use case in writing before proposing a seat/asset expansion, so the ask is grounded in an observed workflow, not an assumed one
expansion_playbooks.customer_ask: confirm which team is now actively using the platform and what workflow they've adopted

expansion_playbooks.trigger: usage growth sustained for 60+ consecutive days without a corresponding entitlement increase
expansion_playbooks.play: surface the entitlement ceiling to the account before they hit it themselves, framed as capacity planning, not an upsell
expansion_playbooks.customer_ask: review current usage against entitled capacity before the ceiling becomes an operational blocker
